name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries encrypt data on Azure resources to extort victims. In cloud environments, this can involve encrypting VM disks using a customer-supplied key with the legitimate Azure Disk Encryption service, deploying ransomware payloads to Azure VMs via VM extensions or Run Command, encrypting files in Azure Storage by overwriting blobs with encrypted content, or rotating Customer Managed Keys (CMK) in Key Vault and denying the storage account access to the new key version. AzureActivity logs disk encryption, VM extension deployments, and Key Vault key operations that precede data lockout.
iocs: AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE' deploying custom script or PowerShell extensions to multiple VMs — ransomware staging
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION' bulk execution across multiple VMs — remote code execution for encryption payload delivery
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/KEYS/VERSIONS/WRITE' creating a new key version followed by rotation of the storage account CMK — storage lockout
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/DISKS/WRITE' with encryption settings modified outside normal change windows
AzureActivity showing Key Vault access policy modified to remove the storage account's key access permissions
AzureActivity bulk 'MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/WRITE' modifying encryption key source from Microsoft-managed to Customer-managed with a new key reference
StorageBlobLogs showing mass 'PutBlob' overwrite operations on existing blobs within a container — file overwrite pattern consistent with encryption ransomware
evasion_variants: Use legitimate Azure Disk Encryption service with attacker-controlled Key Vault key rather than deploying custom encryption malware — avoids AV/EDR detections
Rotate CMK for storage encryption using Azure Key Vault policy rather than custom tooling — appears as routine key rotation in audit logs
Deploy ransomware payload via a VM extension update rather than RunCommand to avoid RunCommand-specific alert rules
Stage encryption tools in an Azure Storage blob before deploying — fewer execution telemetry events than agent-based delivery
Disable Azure Key Vault diagnostics logging before rotating keys to reduce detection window
Perform encryption operations on a weekend or holiday when SOC response times are extended
detection_kql_hints: AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE' | where Properties has_any ('CustomScriptExtension','RunPowerShellScript','EnableAzureDiskEncryption') | summarize count() by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where count_ > 3
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION' | summarize VMsTargeted=dcount(ResourceId) by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m) | where VMsTargeted > 5

AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has 'KEYVAULT/VAULTS/KEYS' and OperationNameValue has 'WRITE' | join kind=inner (AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/WRITE') on Caller | where abs(datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated1)) < 60
StorageBlobLogs | where OperationName == 'PutBlob' | summarize OverwriteCount=count() by AccountName, ContainerName, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m) | where OverwriteCount > 500
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has 'KEYVAULT' and Properties has '"enabled":false' | project TimeGenerated, Caller, OperationNameValue, Properties
